description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and a distinct row of vertical grooves or indentations along one edge. The block appears to be made of a dense, coarse-grained stone, likely sandstone or limestone, with a natural grayish-brown coloration and some areas of lighter and darker shading. The top and sides are irregular, with chipped and broken edges, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements or mechanical wear. The grooved face is the most visually striking feature, possibly indicating it was once part of a larger architectural or structural element. The block is solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it was used as a functional barrier or boundary element in an urban or historical setting.
description: A heavy, weathered stone block with vertical grooves along one edge and an irregular, chipped surface. The coloration is grayish-brown with natural stone texture.
description_view_0: The grooved face of the stone block is clearly visible, showing deep vertical indentations and a rough, chipped upper edge.
description_view_1: A narrow side profile reveals the block's thickness and irregular, jagged edges, with little surface detail visible.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is mostly flat and unadorned, with the grooves still visible along the bottom edge and a rough, natural stone texture.
description_view_3: Another narrow side profile, similar to view 1, showing the uneven thickness and chipped contours of the block.
attribute_location_context: historic urban street or plaza boundary
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set in compacted earth
attribute_weathering_pattern: Chipped corners and edges with some surface discoloration, indicating exposure to weather and wear over time.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Vertical grooves or indentations are present along one edge; no inscriptions or decorative carvings visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely interlocked or aligned with neighboring blocks to form a continuous barrier or boundary.
